name: Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc
summary:
CVE-2024-41570: Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc
This vulnerability is exploited by spoofing a demon agent registration and checkins to open a TCP socket on the teamserver and read/write data from it. This allows attackers to leak origin IPs of teamservers and much more.

When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc as a cataloged Post-Exploitation tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sed -i '/case COMMAND_SOCKET:/,/return true/d' teamserver/pkg/agent/agent.go` as documented upstream for Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `usage: exploit.py [-h] -t TARGET -i IP -p PORT [-A USER_AGENT] [-H HOSTNAME] [-u USERNAME] [-d DOMAIN_NAME]` as documented upstream for Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc.
When chaining Post-Exploitation tools, pair Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Havoc-C2-SSRF-poc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
